sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. عرَمَ to make naked, [“to be naked”], to uncover, whence עָרֹם, עָרֹום which see. Intrans. عَرِمَ to be impudent, spiteful (manifesting one’s malevolent mind).

(2) to be crafty. (Syr. Ethpe. id. ܥܰܪܝܺܡܳܐ, עֲרִימָא crafty, spiteful.) Once found in Kal, 1Sa 23:22.

Hiphil-

(1) to make crafty, Psa 83:4, יַעֲרִימוּ סֹוד “they make their counsel crafty,” they take crafty counsels.

(2) to act craftily, 1Sa 23:22, and, in a good sense, to act prudently, Pro 15:5 19:25 .

Derivatives עָרוּם, עֹרֶם, עָרְמָה

[עָרֹום, עֵירֹם, מַעֲרֻמִּים].

II.

ערם

not used in Kal, cognate to the verbs, אָרַם, הָרַם, רָאַם, רוּם, רָמַם, to be high. (Syr. Pa. to hear up; Arab. عرم to be heaped up. Saad. Exo 15:8 عَرَمَةُ a heap of grain on the threshing floor.)

Niphal, to be heaped up, Exo 15:8.

Derivatives, עֲרֵמָה, עַרְמֹון.
